reviews.text: I tried to reach out to Conrad Manila about this concern, I called thru phone and already talked with different hotel staff members to follow this up but none really devoted time to fix this so I ran out of patience and this left me frustrated. So I am making this review here, hoping something will happen. I booked 2 rooms for an overnight stay 2 weeks ago for my colleagues. To make the transaction swift, I asked the hotel to charge my credit card directly since the mode of payment was “PAY AT THE HOTEL”. I provided a credit card payment authorization form for them to charge the exact amount for accommodation. Then I was informed I it was successfully charged. reviews.title: Very Traumatic
reviews.text: I’m always visiting Manila and I’ve always wanted to try Conrad. My family had the worst and most traumatic experience. Their rooms are very dangerous for kids. Their sofa had no backrest and my daughter hit her head on their very sharp edged window. Ambulance had to take us to the ER where she had a very deep lacerated wound. Worst part is nobody visited us and the top management didnt even reach out to us. The hotel did not even offer to pay for hospital bill and or waive room fees (we got 4 rooms) despite their room hazards. We complained about our breakfast food that had hair and the waiters didnt even care nor give us a complimentary meal, this was a free breakfast but this hotel doesnt seem to care about complaints. Their lifeguard in the pool was very rude also to us when my kids wanted to swim and we had to borrow towels to bring to our rooms. But worst of all, nobody from the hotel checked on my daughter!! This hotel is one of the worst! Very dangerous and zero service.

reviews.title: 2-Star Service at a 5-Star premium
reviews.positives: The Hotel is in a very good location, and it’s easy to have a staycation. Generally the amenities are good, though the pool was a little underwhelming.
reviews.negatives:
Apparently they don’t really ensure that the guest experience is first since some of the room features did not work- such as being able to shut off all the lights, and the bidet has an issue where it might leak if you are unaware. 

Also you have to keep asking for water since they only provide you with three bottles, and this gets annoying in the evening. 

Plus the Keycard conked out twice. It’s the small things that make the stay feel like you aren’t overpaying, and the Conrad flubbed hard.

reviews.title: Nightmare Experience & Extremely Poor Customer Service
reviews.text: Upon arrival in the hotel, the manager approached to inform me the hotel was OVERBOOKED and that there are no available rooms  and that she has no option but to move me to Lanson Place (LOWER star and UNKNOWN hotel 2 blocks away) for 1 night. This is despite booking my reservation (for 2 nights) more than 3 months advance. I responded that we will not agree since I specifically booked this hotel due to a wedding happening here and that she should find a way to give me any room in the hotel. I even asked her if I am the last person to check-in  to which she replied that I am not. After multiple rounds of the same conversation (each round increasing my anger and frustration), she finally said that she will see what she can do. After a few minutes, she came back with a room but guilt-tripped us by saying that she will just offer the Lanson Place room to those who will check in after.  Before finalizing the check-in she shamelessly took another jab and offered us the room in Lanson and emphasized that the room has complimentary breakfast. I  don't know what she was thinking because I was obviously so furious already! At the end of it all, she just gave us our regular room without any effort to lessen our anger. My friend who was having his wedding in Conrad also had multiple mishaps with the hotel as well. The poor customer service and calibre of management is inexcusable, frustrating is worst than a motel. DO YOURSELVES A FAVOR AND DO NOT STAY IN THIS HOTEL.

reviews.title: Not every impressed with the type of service we got during our stay, location was the only plus
reviews.positives: proximity to the nearby areas like MOA, Okada, IKEA as well as restaurants and shops makes it a great stay
reviews.negatives: One of the rooms we booked was not ready and in fact we got to use the room at 5pm, it also took them 40 minutes to bring the luggages up despite us calling 3x already..checkin line was unusually long and the guard in charge wanted me to fall in line even when I just needed an extra key to the room. We've stayed at other 5 star hotels that gave greater and better service.

reviews.title: Don't book for Executive lounge access!
reviews.text: Just book for a regular room with breakfasts.  Suites or club rooms with lounge access offer 2 star food quality, and guests would feel so unwelcome..as if they will  run out of food as they immediately close the sliding door of food when its time out for breakfast and cocktails. And if you are with older children, always put them as adults because even if you opt to choose 17yo as child at a higher cost booking, you will still be charged an add on 1,800 pesos for breakfast each child. And even if they allow 4 people in the room with one king bed, they will not prepare 4 beddings and if you request extra bed you will be charged on top of what you paid.  You may choose nearby hotels, like Okada with lounge access, so worth the pay! Just take grab taxi if you need to go to SM mall or convention center.

reviews.title: It's only trappings of a 5 star hotel but not the actual quality of one
reviews.text:
Has the trappings of a 5 star hotel only but not the actual quality of one. First thing first, we got to the hotel late around midnight. When we asked where to park nearest to the hotel entrance, we were led to the other end furthest from the hotel entrance. It was off to a brilliant start. Check-in was fine but afterwards we didn't have anyone to help us with our luggage. We had to drag them up ourselves. Then, it rolled further downhill as we realised no one gave us the wifi password. It took 2 tries and ages and ages of waiting before someone would answer our call with regards to the password. When we got our information, lo and behold, we were being asked for payment even though our room included a free wifi. Another call and another long wait and the problem was solved. The kicker was when in middle of the night, we woke up because the air conditioner just shut-offed and the room had gotten hot. Frankly, we were too tired to complain and we needed to go but to say the least, the whole time we freshened up and changed the air conditioner was dead.
Breakfast was ho hum. It might have the spread of a five star but it certainly didn't have the quality. I can go in detail as to why it unacceptable. For example, one of the dim sums was just made with flour extenders, you know those things you find in cheap dim sum stalls in the mall fast food. It's fine if you want a cheap meal, but its certainly not what anyone should find in the 5 star hotel, much less one who supposedly has a good chines restaurant.
The worst was at the end of the stay, we had a approved late check out. When we came back to our room after lunch, way before our check out time, we got locked out. We had to find a guard who had to call the lobby and we had to wait for a long long time at the hallway for someone to open our door. And the reason why we were locked out because the key scrambles itself after 12 noon on the check out day. Now if you have a late check out, expect to be locked in your room.

reviews.title: Could be better
reviews.positives:
For a 5 star hotel I expected flawless execution but surprisingly that was not the case. Here is my positive and negative experience for the Conrad Manila. The goal of this review is to provide feedback to the hotel so they can improve the experience of guest that are traveling solo.

Positive Experience:
+Room was nicely decorated
+Free Nespresso Coffee and two bottles of water
+I was able to check in once room was cleaned.
+ Late checkout
+ Internet was perfect!  Able to work remotely from various locations in the hotel.
+ Staff at C Lounge was excellent.
+Connected to Mall of Asia perfect if the weather is bad.
+Requested a high floor on quiet side of hotel.  I got that! Thank you!

Positive & Negative Experience:
-+ I checked in early and was told they would call me once room was ready.  Never got the call. Lucky I went to the counter again to follow-up.
-+ Hit or miss on the restaurant staff at Brasserie On 3. The hostess and majority of staff was great at the restaurant.  They explained the buffet selection. But I had to ask for ice water twice before I received it.
-+ The need to x-Ray my backpack at every entrances. Both a positive and negative experience.
-+ Checkout you have to lineup.  So when the line is long expect to wait. However, a lady named Clair helped me after I explained I needed to be in a meeting and was running late because of the long wait. She was amazing and followed through till I was able to checkout.
-+ Majority of staff members had excellent customer service and where friendly and helpful.  However there were a couple that did not seem happy to be there.
reviews.negatives:
Absolutely negative experience:

- Shower area in room 5054 smelled of pee.  Even when washing it down.
- Concierge had placed my drone in a vault (not by choice).  When I got downstairs to pick it up. The box was sitting on top of the counter in plain site.  For high values item that is placed in your hotels vault please keep it behind the counter for all your customers.
- Asked for a bucket of ice, never got it.  I guess reception got to busy.  Not important but if you ask for something that is important make sure to remind reception otherwise you might not get what you requested.
- The checkout process for guest can be greatly improved. Your guest checking out should not have to stand in line for over 30 minutes just to checkout.

reviews.title: My wife and I stayed in Conrad Manila Hotel for 3 nights. We both give this place a 4/5 stars.
reviews.positives:
My wife and I stayed in Conrad Manila Hotel for 3 nights. We both give this place a 4/5 stars. 
The hotel reached out via email to confirm our early morning arrival at 0300 and upgraded our suite to accommodate us even better given that we paid an extra day to compensate our arrival window. That itself made the experience personable. From the food to the hotel staff, we have no complaints at all compared to the other 5 star hotels. We stayed on the 7th floor, executive lounge, and their bathroom setup was incredible-- highly recommend! The hotel view from the room is subpar. There was construction in front of the pool and across the Bay. They’re building an extension of the mall that ruins the whole view. Furthermore, the smell if you step outside the hotel is horrendous. Just an absolute pungent smell coming from the Bay. You can also smell this if you’re in the pool. It’s pretty bad. The hotel has a ton of food and shopping options given that it’s literally connected to the Mall. Also, IKEA is nearby, two blocks down, if you would like to check that out and walk around. We recommend this place over Grand Hyatt Hotel in BGC which we also stayed at for 4 nights.

But your pastries in buffet are good. Toilets are clean. Rooms are clean.

Breakfast buffet isn’t worth getting.  I would assume the lunch and dinner is just as bad.
reviews.response:
Dear Cory,

Thank you for sharing your feedback. We truly appreciate your kind words and regret to hear about your experience.

Following a thorough review, I am pleased to confirm that our team promptly arranged a room transfer upon learning about your concerns. I have also shared your comments with the team for review and resolution, where appropriate.

As mentioned in our pre-arrival letter, we are delighted to share that a vibrant new city is rising along Manila Bay. This development, led by the local government and private partners, is already underway and may affect the bay’s picturesque views. Rest assured, we remain committed to delivering experiences that reflect warm Filipino hospitality and the excellence that defines our brand throughout every guest’s stay.

We are also exploring additional ways to communicate this more effectively with all our guests.

Thank you for taking the time to bring your concerns to our attention. We look forward to welcoming you back to Conrad Man

reviews.title: Horrible value for money
reviews.text:
1. The cost of stay to be paid after taxes is different from what is quoted online through agoda (with taxes included), a difference of php 2-3000 per night! Hence why they do not have the prepaid option on agoda which would allow people to opt for better value choices. It is best to call ahead to hotel and confirm total cost payable so you are not caught out. 
2. For the same price paid per night here, you can get a spacious 2 bedroom apartment in another 5 star hotel in Ayala Center area., where the shopping experience is much better (Glorietta & Greenbelt malls)
3. I booked a room inclusive of breakfast for 3 adults and 1 child (child is defined as aged under 18 according to agoda) yet had to pay extra for the breakfast for the child instead as the hotel definition of child is aged 12 and above. (Oddly, the hotel only offers 3 adult breakfasts for a 4 pax per room stay). Why just not include the 4th breakfast too if you already charge for 4 people to stay in that room??

reviews.title: Below Expectations for a Conrad Stay
reviews.text:
I stayed at this hotel for two nights and unfortunately had a disappointing experience, starting with check-in.

I arrived one hour late because the hotel had no available parking. There was an event at the arena beside the property, and the hotel shares parking with the mall, which is open to the public. When I called the front desk for assistance, I was simply told that both self-parking and valet were full. No alternatives, no guidance, just “sorry.” For a hotel stay I had already fully paid for, it was extremely frustrating to be left on my own to solve something as basic as parking.

The food was another major letdown. The breakfast buffet is absolutely not worth it- nothing was terrible, but everything was very mediocre and forgettable. Room service was even worse. The spaghetti bolognese was bland, and the wagyu burger was dry, flavorless, and honestly tasted like cardboard. Very disappointing, especially given the pricing.

Overall, the lack of parking support and poor food quality made this stay far below expectations. I wouldn’t stay here again.

reviews.title: Not staying again
reviews.text:
Hotel deposit of Php5K ($90) is required upon check-in which was NOT stated in the booking fine print, and even if the executive room reservation was fully paid in advance - Php23K ($410). If guests have only a debit card, the deposit amount if unconsumed will be refunded after 1 month+…insanely long. 

Days before the check-in, we were communicating with the reservation officer informing him that this stay was for a 50th anniversary celebration but he never mentioned about the hotel policy regarding the deposit. Since this was paid in full, the celebrants didn’t bring much cash and credit card. It was such a hassle for a senior elderly couple to find a working ATM machine to withdraw cash, the defective BDO ATM machine in the complex didn’t help at all.

They also did NOT allow the points to be added to the Hilton Honors membership of the couple son’s who booked and paid for the stay as a gift to his parents.

Their band-aid solution: room upgrade, fruits in room, 1:30pm check-in, 2pm check-out

reviews.title: Poor Front Desk Service (lack of proper training)
reviews.text:
I was so excited for Conrad Hotel (beside Mall of Asia) to open,  only to get disappointed. I will no longer emphasize on the smell of the room toilet (i duno what causes its foul smell, coz it looks clean). I will just share my experience with the front desk staff. 
On the day of our checkout, i called the front desk and asked if it is possible to check out at 1pm (an hour late), or even a little earlier, since my 2 year old son was still sleeping (he got tired swimming the previous day). But to my surprise, that was the first time i heard a hotel front desk staff telling me that categorically "IT IS NOT ALLOWED". Again, i asked for a little consideration, but to no avail.. I don't know..I could be wrong there, but you know, in my almost 20 years of staying at different hotels in many parts of the world, that was really the first time i heard a flat denial coming from a front desk staff. I even told the lady, if it's okay with her (or Conrad) if I post a review regarding our conversation, and she said she won't mind (or something to that effect). So here it is.
Anyway, i really don't know what will change my mind and book again at Conrad Mall of Asia, Pasay City...So disappointing...

Some staffs are friendly, some are not and will simply ignore you.

reviews.title: Below Expectations for a Conrad Stay
reviews.text:
I stayed at this hotel for two nights and unfortunately had a disappointing experience, starting with check-in.

I arrived one hour late because the hotel had no available parking. There was an event at the arena beside the property, and the hotel shares parking with the mall, which is open to the public. When I called the front desk for assistance, I was simply told that both self-parking and valet were full. No alternatives, no guidance, just “sorry.” For a hotel stay I had already fully paid for, it was extremely frustrating to be left on my own to solve something as basic as parking.

The food was another major letdown. The breakfast buffet is absolutely not worth it. Nothing was terrible, but everything was very mediocre and forgettable. Room service was even worse. The spaghetti bolognese was bland, and the wagyu burger was dry, flavorless, and honestly tasted like cardboard. Very disappointing, especially given the pricing.

Overall, the lack of parking support and poor food quality made this stay far below expectations.

Slowest check-in process I've experienced at an upscale hotel.
I feel they intentionally like to keep a queue to seem exclusive and premium.
Staff took a strangely long time to get check me in despite having fully paid in advance and arriving at 3pm sharp - staff made it seem like he put a lot of work in to being able to "find me a room" at the time I arrived like I should be thankful he got my room ready at the time it was supposed to be ready anyway - it should have been ready in advance.
Also had a matter I needed help with and dialed 0 from the room. Had to call them three times for them to communicate something to the front desk. Just an overall unreliable and slow experience from the staff.
I don't think I'll be staying here again

reviews.title: Convention
reviews.text:
The hotel was adjacent to the convention center which made it very convenient and easy for me to attend the conferences. We had an early check-in which they allowed without any additional fees. There was a minor inconvenience during our first night, which they addressed promptly. We were upgraded to a 1 bedroom suite to which we are very thankful. My son enjoyed the pool and bathtub, while my husband appreciated the large desk which he used as a makeshift office. We had a late checkout, to which we had to pay extra, but I did not mind because the service was superb. Thank you to Ms. Crizel Cheng and Ms. Nina Yung, the duty managers and the rest of the staff who handled our issue. I would also recommend their spa, might be on the expensive side, but it's nice to indulge every once in a while. Ms. Jennie, my therapist, took care of me really well. 

Overall, we had a great stay at Conrad Manila. I'll probably book here again for next year's convention.
reviews.response:

Sincerely,
Rupert Hallam
